page: Amazon Web Services m8idb.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by Intel processors operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 192 virtual CPUs mapped to 96 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core. The CPU resources are dedicated, ensuring consistent compute allocation. The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

For local storage requirements, the m8idb.48xlarge includes 11,400 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. This large-capacity local storage is bundled directly with the instance, offering high-speed, low-latency data access. The network configuration delivers a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps, supporting high-throughput data transfers. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the combination of dense dedicated compute, substantial memory, and massive integrated NVMe storage reduces the need for external block storage attachments, optimizing resource density for data-intensive workloads.

The resource profile of the m8idb.48xlarge makes it suitable for large-scale general-purpose workloads, enterprise databases, and data-intensive applications that require high-speed local scratch space. The lack of dedicated GPU hardware makes it unsuitable for accelerated graphics or heavy machine learning training workloads. It is best utilized for high-throughput web serving, large-scale CI/CD pipelines, ETL processes, and database hosting.
description: Amazon Web Services m8idb.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 192 dedicated vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by 3.9 GHz Intel processors on the x86_64 architecture. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 768.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance includes 11,400 GB of bundled NVMe SSD local storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. This combination of high-density compute, substantial memory, and massive local storage capacity makes it highly efficient for workloads requiring rapid local data access without the overhead of external storage. It is suitable for enterprise databases, large-scale data processing, ETL pipelines, and high-throughput web serving.
